title.raw: 《呂不韋列傳》 Biography of Master Lü Buwei

sentences.zh: 呂不韋者，陽翟大賈人也。

translations.literal: Lü Buwei was a great merchant of Yangzhai.
translations.idiomatic: Lü Buwei was a wealthy merchant from the city of Yangzhai.

sentences.zh: 往來販賤賣貴，家累千金。

translations.literal: He traveled about buying cheap and selling dear, and his family accumulated a thousand catties of gold.
translations.idiomatic: He traveled widely, buying low and selling high, until his household had amassed a fortune of a thousand catties of gold.

translations.literal: Lü Buwei was a great merchant from Yangdi. He traveled trading, buying cheap selling dear, household accumulated thousand jin gold.

sentences.zh: 秦昭王四十年，太子死。

translations.literal: In the fortieth year of King Zhao of Qin, the crown prince died.
translations.idiomatic: In the fortieth year of the reign of King Zhao of Qin, the crown prince died.

sentences.zh: 其四十二年，以其次子安國君為太子。

translations.literal: In his forty-second year, he made his second son, Lord Anguo, the crown prince.
translations.idiomatic: Two years later, the king designated his second son, Lord Anguo, as the new crown prince.

sentences.zh: 安國君有子二十餘人。

translations.literal: Lord Anguo had more than twenty sons.
translations.idiomatic: Lord Anguo had more than twenty sons.

sentences.zh: 安國君有所甚愛姬，立以為正夫人，號曰華陽夫人。

translations.literal: Lord Anguo had a concubine whom he greatly loved, and he made her his principal wife, titled Lady Huayang.
translations.idiomatic: Lord Anguo deeply favored one of his concubines and elevated her to the rank of principal wife, bestowing upon her the title of Lady Huayang.

sentences.zh: 華陽夫人無子。

translations.literal: Lady Huayang had no sons.
translations.idiomatic: Lady Huayang, however, had borne no sons.

sentences.zh: 安國君中男名子楚，子楚母曰夏姬，毋愛。

translations.literal: Lord Anguo's middle son was named Zichu; Zichu's mother was called Lady Xia, and she was not favored.
translations.idiomatic: One of Lord Anguo's middle sons was named Zichu. His mother, Lady Xia, had long fallen out of favor.

sentences.zh: 子楚為秦質子於趙。

translations.literal: Zichu served as a Qin hostage prince in Zhao.
translations.idiomatic: Zichu had been sent to the state of Zhao as a hostage prince on behalf of Qin.

sentences.zh: 秦數攻趙，趙不甚禮子楚。

translations.literal: Qin repeatedly attacked Zhao, and Zhao did not treat Zichu with much courtesy.
translations.idiomatic: Since Qin had repeatedly waged war against Zhao, the Zhao court showed Zichu little courtesy.

translations.literal: Qin King Zhao fortieth year, crown prince died. His forty-second year, made his second son Anguo Jun crown prince. Anguo Jun had sons twenty plus persons. Anguo Jun had a much loved concubine, established as proper wife, titled Huayang Madame. Huayang Madame had no sons. Anguo Jun middle son named Zichu, Zichu mother called Xia Ji, not favored. Zichu served as Qin hostage in Zhao. Qin repeatedly attacked Zhao, Zhao not much honored Zichu.

sentences.zh: 子楚，秦諸庶孽孫，質於諸侯，車乘進用不饒，居處困，不得意。

translations.literal: Zichu, a grandson of Qin born of a concubine, held hostage among the feudal lords, lacked carriages, horses, and provisions, lived in straitened circumstances, and was not content.
translations.idiomatic: Zichu was merely one of the many grandsons of the Qin royal house born to concubines. Held hostage among the feudal lords, he lacked carriages, horses, and provisions. He lived in straitened circumstances, frustrated and discontented.

sentences.zh: 呂不韋賈邯鄲，見而憐之，曰「此奇貨可居」。

translations.literal: Lü Buwei was trading in Handan, saw him and pitied him, saying, "This is a rare commodity worth investing in."
translations.idiomatic: When Lü Buwei came to Handan on business, he encountered Zichu and took pity on him. "Here," he said to himself, "is a rare commodity worth investing in."

sentences.zh: 乃往見子楚，說曰：「吾能大子之門。」

translations.literal: He then went to see Zichu and persuaded him, saying, "I can elevate your house."
translations.idiomatic: He went to call on Zichu and said, "I can raise the standing of your house."

sentences.zh: 子楚笑曰：「且自大君之門，而乃大吾門！」

translations.literal: Zichu laughed and said, "You should first elevate your own house before trying to elevate mine!"
translations.idiomatic: Zichu laughed. "You had better raise the standing of your own house first, before you worry about mine!"

sentences.zh: 呂不韋曰：「子不知也，吾門待子門而大。」

translations.literal: Lü Buwei said, "You do not understand. My house's rise depends on your house's rise."
translations.idiomatic: Lü Buwei replied, "You do not understand. The standing of my house depends upon the standing of yours."

sentences.zh: 子楚心知所謂，乃引與坐，深語。

translations.literal: Zichu understood what he meant, so he invited him to sit and they spoke at length.
translations.idiomatic: Zichu grasped his meaning at once and invited him to sit down, and the two men talked at length.

sentences.zh: 呂不韋曰：「秦王老矣，安國君得為太子。

translations.literal: Lü Buwei said, "The King of Qin is old, and Lord Anguo has become crown prince.
translations.idiomatic: Lü Buwei said, "The King of Qin is growing old, and Lord Anguo has been made crown prince.

sentences.zh: 竊聞安國君愛幸華陽夫人，華陽夫人無子，能立適嗣者獨華陽夫人耳。

translations.literal: I have secretly heard that Lord Anguo loves and favors Lady Huayang; Lady Huayang has no sons; the only one who can establish the legitimate heir is Lady Huayang alone.
translations.idiomatic: I have heard that Lord Anguo dotes on Lady Huayang, yet Lady Huayang has no sons of her own. She alone has the power to determine who will be named the legitimate heir.

sentences.zh: 今子兄弟二十餘人，子又居中，不甚見幸，久質諸侯。

translations.literal: Now you have more than twenty brothers, and you are in the middle rank, not much favored, and have long been a hostage among the feudal lords.
translations.idiomatic: You are one of more than twenty brothers, ranked somewhere in the middle, and you have never been particularly favored. You have been kept as a hostage among the feudal lords for a long time.

sentences.zh: 即大王薨，安國君立為王，則子毋幾得與長子及諸子旦暮在前者爭為太子矣。」

translations.literal: When the great king dies and Lord Anguo becomes king, you will have little chance of competing for the position of crown prince against the eldest son and those sons who attend him morning and evening."
translations.idiomatic: When the king dies and Lord Anguo ascends the throne, you will have no chance of competing for the position of heir against the eldest son and those brothers who attend upon him morning and evening."

sentences.zh: 子楚曰：「然。

translations.literal: Zichu said, "That is so.
translations.idiomatic: Zichu said, "You are right.

sentences.zh: 為之柰何？」

translations.literal: What can be done about it?"
translations.idiomatic: But what can be done?"

sentences.zh: 呂不韋曰：「子貧，客於此，非有以奉獻於親及結賓客也。

translations.literal: Lü Buwei said, "You are poor and a guest here, with nothing to offer as gifts to your relatives or to win over retainers.
translations.idiomatic: Lü Buwei said, "You are poor and living as a stranger here, with no means to send gifts to your family or cultivate a circle of retainers.

sentences.zh: 不韋雖貧，請以千金為子西游，事安國君及華陽夫人，立子為適嗣。」

translations.literal: Though Buwei is not wealthy, I beg to spend a thousand catties of gold to travel west on your behalf, to serve Lord Anguo and Lady Huayang, and to establish you as the legitimate heir."
translations.idiomatic: Though I am not a rich man, allow me to spend a thousand catties of gold to travel west to Qin on your behalf. I will attend upon Lord Anguo and Lady Huayang and secure your appointment as the legitimate heir."

sentences.zh: 子楚乃頓首曰：「必如君策，請得分秦國與君共之。」

translations.literal: Zichu then kowtowed and said, "If your plan succeeds, I wish to divide the state of Qin and share it with you."
translations.idiomatic: Zichu bowed his head to the ground and said, "If your plan succeeds, I swear I shall share the kingdom of Qin with you."

translations.literal: Zichu was Qin various concubine descendants, hostage among feudal lords, chariot riding expenses not plentiful, dwelling poor, not pleased. Lü Buwei traded Handan, saw and pitied him, said 'This rare goods can store.' Thus went saw Zichu, persuaded saying: 'I can enlarge your gate.' Zichu laughed saying: 'First enlarge your lord's gate, then enlarge my gate!' Lü Buwei said: 'You do not know. My gate waits for your gate to enlarge.' Zichu heart knew meaning, thus led him sit, deeply spoke. Lü Buwei said: 'Qin king aged, Anguo Jun can become crown prince. I secretly heard Anguo Jun loves favors Huayang Madame, Huayang Madame has no sons, can establish proper heir is only Huayang Madame. Now your brothers twenty plus persons, you again middle position, not much favored, long hostage among lords. If great king dies, Anguo Jun establishes as king, then you few chance to contend with eldest son and various sons day night before as crown prince.' Zichu said: 'Yes. Handle how?' Lü Buwei said: 'You poor, guest here, no means to present to parents and tie guests. Buwei though poor, request with thousand jin gold for you west travel, serve Anguo Jun and Huayang Madame, establish you as proper heir.' Zichu thus bowed head: 'Must like your plan, please divide Qin state share with you.'

sentences.zh: 呂不韋乃以五百金與子楚，為進用，結賓客；

translations.literal: Lü Buwei then gave five hundred catties of gold to Zichu, for his expenses and to cultivate retainers;
translations.idiomatic: Lü Buwei then gave Zichu five hundred catties of gold to cover his daily expenses and to cultivate a circle of retainers.

sentences.zh: 而復以五百金買奇物玩好，自奉而西游秦，求見華陽夫人姊，而皆以其物獻華陽夫人。

translations.literal: With another five hundred catties of gold, he bought rare treasures and curiosities, and carried them himself westward to Qin, where he sought an audience with Lady Huayang's elder sister and presented all the objects to Lady Huayang.
translations.idiomatic: With the remaining five hundred catties, he purchased rare treasures and curiosities, then traveled west to Qin himself. There he sought an audience with Lady Huayang's elder sister and presented all the objects as gifts to Lady Huayang.

sentences.zh: 因言子楚賢智，結諸侯賓客遍天下，常曰「楚也以夫人為天，日夜泣思太子及夫人」。

translations.literal: He then spoke of Zichu's wisdom and virtue, and how he had cultivated guests and retainers among the feudal lords throughout the realm, and always said, "Zichu regards the Lady as his heaven, and weeps day and night in longing for the crown prince and the Lady."
translations.idiomatic: He spoke of Zichu's wisdom and virtue, of how the young prince had befriended retainers and guests across all the feudal states, and how Zichu always said, "I look upon Lady Huayang as my heaven, and day and night I weep with longing for the crown prince and the Lady."

sentences.zh: 夫人大喜。

translations.literal: The Lady was greatly pleased.
translations.idiomatic: Lady Huayang was overjoyed.

sentences.zh: 不韋因使其姊說夫人曰：「吾聞之，以色事人者，色衰而愛弛。

translations.literal: Buwei then had her sister persuade the Lady, saying, "I have heard that those who serve others through beauty will find that when their beauty fades, love will slacken."
translations.idiomatic: Lü Buwei then had the elder sister counsel Lady Huayang, saying, "I have heard it said that she who wins a man's love through beauty will find that love fading when her beauty fades."

sentences.zh: 今夫人事太子，甚愛而無子，不以此時蚤自結於諸子中賢孝者，舉立以為適而子之，夫在則重尊，夫百歲之後，所子者為王，終不失勢，此所謂一言而萬世之利也。

translations.literal: Now the Lady serves the crown prince and is greatly loved, but she has no sons. If she does not at this time hasten to bind herself to the most worthy and filial among his sons, raising one as the legitimate heir and adopting him as her own, then while her husband lives she will be doubly honored, and after his death, the one she has adopted will be king and she will never lose power. This is what is called gaining ten thousand generations of benefit with a single word.
translations.idiomatic: You now serve the crown prince and enjoy his deep love, yet you have borne him no sons. If you do not seize this moment to choose the worthiest and most filial of his sons, establish him as the legitimate heir, and adopt him as your own, then you will have squandered your chance. While your husband lives, you will be doubly honored. After he passes, the son you have chosen will be king, and you will never lose your position. This is what they call gaining ten thousand generations of benefit through a single decision.

sentences.zh: 不以繁華時樹本，即色衰愛弛後，雖欲開一語，尚可得乎？

translations.literal: If you do not plant your roots while you flourish, once your beauty fades and love slackens, even if you wished to speak a single word, would you still be able to?
translations.idiomatic: If you do not plant your roots during your time of glory, then once your beauty fades and his love wanes, you will not be able to utter even a single word of influence.

sentences.zh: 今子楚賢，而自知中男也，次不得為適，其母又不得幸，自附夫人，夫人誠以此時拔以為適，夫人則竟世有寵於秦矣。」

translations.literal: Now Zichu is a worthy man, and he himself knows he is a middle son, whose rank does not entitle him to be heir, and whose mother is not favored. He will attach himself to the Lady. If the Lady truly takes this opportunity to elevate him as the legitimate heir, the Lady will enjoy favor in Qin for the rest of her life."
translations.idiomatic: Now Zichu is a worthy man who knows full well that as a middle son with no claim to the succession and an unfavored mother, he must depend upon your patronage. If you take this opportunity to elevate him as the legitimate heir, he will be devoted to you, and you will enjoy power and favor in Qin for the rest of your days."

sentences.zh: 華陽夫人以為然，承太子閒，從容言子楚質於趙者絕賢，來往者皆稱譽之。

translations.literal: Lady Huayang thought this was right, and when the crown prince was at leisure, she casually mentioned that Zichu, the hostage in Zhao, was extraordinarily worthy, and that all who came and went praised him highly.
translations.idiomatic: Lady Huayang agreed with this counsel. When she found the crown prince at leisure, she brought up the subject of Zichu with studied casualness, remarking that the hostage in Zhao was said to be extraordinarily talented, and that all travelers who had met him spoke highly of his character.

sentences.zh: 乃因涕泣曰：「妾幸得充後宮，不幸無子，願得子楚立以為適嗣，以託妾身。」

translations.literal: Then she wept and said, "I was fortunate enough to fill a place in the rear palace, but unfortunately I have no son. I wish to have Zichu established as the legitimate heir, so that I may entrust my person to him."
translations.idiomatic: Then she began to weep and said, "I have been fortunate enough to serve in your household, yet I have been cursed with no son of my own. I beg you, let Zichu be established as the legitimate heir, so that I may have someone to depend upon in my old age."

sentences.zh: 安國君許之，乃與夫人刻玉符，約以為適嗣。

translations.literal: Lord Anguo agreed, and together with the Lady he engraved a jade tally, pledging to make Zichu the legitimate heir.
translations.idiomatic: Lord Anguo consented, and he and Lady Huayang carved a jade tally together, sealing the pledge that Zichu would be named the legitimate heir.

sentences.zh: 安國君及夫人因厚餽遺子楚，而請呂不韋傅之，子楚以此名譽益盛於諸侯。

translations.literal: Lord Anguo and the Lady thereupon sent generous gifts to Zichu, and invited Lü Buwei to be his tutor. Zichu's reputation therefore grew ever greater among the feudal lords.
translations.idiomatic: Lord Anguo and Lady Huayang then sent generous gifts to Zichu and appointed Lü Buwei as his tutor. With this patronage, Zichu's reputation grew ever more distinguished among the feudal lords.

translations.literal: Lü Buwei thus with five hundred jin gold gave Zichu, for expenses, tie guests; Again with five hundred jin gold bought rare objects curios, personally carried west travel Qin, seek see Huayang Madame sister, all with objects presented Huayang Madame. Thus spoke Zichu worthy wise, ties feudal lord guests fill world, often said 'Zichu takes Madame as heaven, day night weep think crown prince and Madame.' Madame greatly pleased. Buwei thus made her sister persuade Madame saying: 'I heard, serve others with beauty, beauty declines love relaxes. Now Madame serves crown prince, much loved yet no sons, not with this time early self-attach to various sons' worthy filial ones, recommend establish as proper to son him, husband alive then heavy honored, husband hundred years after, sonned becomes king, end not lose power, this called one word ten thousand generations benefit. Not with flourishing time plant roots, then beauty declines love relaxes after, though wish open one word, still obtainable? Now Zichu worthy, self knows middle son, cannot become proper, mother again not favored, self-attaches Madame, Madame truly with this time pull establish as proper, Madame then compete lifetime favored in Qin.' Huayang Madame considered correct, seized crown prince leisure, leisurely spoke Zichu hostage in Zhao extremely worthy, passers all praise him. Thus because wept saying: 'Concubine fortunately fill rear palace, unfortunately no sons, wish Zichu establish as proper heir, to entrust concubine body.' Anguo Jun permitted, thus with Madame engraved jade tally, agreed as proper heir. Anguo Jun and Madame thus thick gifts sent Zichu, requested Lü Buwei tutor him, Zichu with this fame reputation increasingly flourishing among feudal lords.

sentences.zh: 呂不韋取邯鄲諸姬絕好善舞者與居，知有身。

translations.literal: Lü Buwei took as his consort one of the most beautiful and accomplished dancers among the women of Handan, and learned that she was with child.
translations.idiomatic: Lü Buwei had taken as his consort a woman of Handan renowned for her extraordinary beauty and skill in dance, and she had become pregnant.

sentences.zh: 子楚從不韋飲，見而說之，因起為壽，請之。

translations.literal: Zichu was drinking with Buwei, saw her and was delighted, and rose to toast his host, then asked for her.
translations.idiomatic: One day while drinking with Lü Buwei, Zichu caught sight of the woman and was captivated. He raised his cup to toast his host and then asked to have her.

sentences.zh: 呂不韋怒，念業已破家為子楚，欲以釣奇，乃遂獻其姬。

translations.literal: Lü Buwei was angry, but reflecting that he had already ruined his family's fortune for Zichu's sake and wishing to use this to land an extraordinary prize, he presented his concubine.
translations.idiomatic: Lü Buwei was furious, but then he reflected that he had already spent his entire fortune on Zichu's behalf and hoped to reap an extraordinary return from the investment. So he presented his concubine to Zichu.

sentences.zh: 姬自匿有身，至大期時，生子政。

translations.literal: The concubine concealed the fact that she was with child; when the full term came, she gave birth to a son, Zheng.
translations.idiomatic: The woman concealed the fact that she was already pregnant. When her time came, she gave birth to a son, whom they named Zheng.

sentences.zh: 子楚遂立姬為夫人。

translations.literal: Zichu thereupon made the concubine his principal wife.
translations.idiomatic: Zichu then made her his principal wife.

translations.literal: Lü Buwei took Handan various beauties extremely good skilled dance with lived, knew had pregnancy. Zichu from Buwei drank, saw pleased, thus rose toast, requested her. Lü Buwei angered, thought already ruined family for Zichu, wished to fish rare, thus presented his beauty. Beauty self hid pregnancy, to great period time, bore son Zheng. Zichu thus established beauty as Madame.

sentences.zh: 秦昭王五十年，使王齮圍邯鄲，急，趙欲殺子楚。

translations.literal: In the fiftieth year of King Zhao of Qin, he sent Wang Yi to besiege Handan. The situation was desperate, and Zhao wanted to kill Zichu.
translations.idiomatic: In the fiftieth year of King Zhao of Qin's reign, the king sent the general Wang Yi to besiege Handan. As the siege tightened, the state of Zhao moved to execute Zichu.

sentences.zh: 子楚與呂不韋謀，行金六百斤予守者吏，得脫，亡赴秦軍，遂以得歸。

translations.literal: Zichu plotted with Lü Buwei, gave six hundred catties of gold to the officials guarding him, managed to escape, fled to the Qin army, and thereby returned home.
translations.idiomatic: Zichu and Lü Buwei hatched a plan. They bribed the officials guarding him with six hundred catties of gold. Zichu escaped, fled to the Qin army lines, and was able to make his way back home.

sentences.zh: 趙欲殺子楚妻子，子楚夫人趙豪家女也，得匿，以故母子竟得活。

translations.literal: Zhao wanted to kill Zichu's wife and son, but Zichu's wife was a daughter of a powerful Zhao family and managed to hide, and thus mother and son survived.
translations.idiomatic: The state of Zhao intended to kill Zichu's wife and son. But Zichu's wife was the daughter of a powerful Zhao family and managed to go into hiding. Through her family's protection, mother and son survived.

sentences.zh: 秦昭王五十六年，薨，太子安國君立為王，華陽夫人為王后，子楚為太子。

translations.literal: In the fifty-sixth year of King Zhao of Qin, he died. The crown prince, Lord Anguo, was made king, Lady Huayang became queen, and Zichu became crown prince.
translations.idiomatic: In the fifty-sixth year of his reign, King Zhao of Qin died. Lord Anguo, the crown prince, ascended the throne. Lady Huayang was made queen, and Zichu was named crown prince.

sentences.zh: 趙亦奉子楚夫人及子政歸秦。

translations.literal: Zhao also sent Zichu's wife and son Zheng back to Qin.
translations.idiomatic: The state of Zhao then escorted Zichu's wife and his son Zheng back to Qin.

translations.literal: Qin King Zhao fiftieth year, sent Wang Ji besiege Handan, urgent, Zhao wished kill Zichu. Zichu with Lü Buwei planned, gave gold six hundred jin to guard officials, got escape, fled join Qin army, thus able return. Zhao wished kill Zichu wife children, Zichu Madame Zhao powerful family girl, got hide, thus mother child finally survived. Qin King Zhao fifty-sixth year, died, crown prince Anguo Jun established as king, Huayang Madame as queen, Zichu as crown prince. Zhao also sent Zichu Madame and Zi Zheng return Qin.

sentences.zh: 秦王立一年，薨，謚為孝文王。

translations.literal: The King of Qin reigned for one year, died, and was given the posthumous title King Xiaowen.
translations.idiomatic: The new King of Qin reigned for only a year before he died. He was given the posthumous title King Xiaowen.

sentences.zh: 太子子楚代立，是為莊襄王。

translations.literal: The crown prince Zichu succeeded him; this was King Zhuangxiang.
translations.idiomatic: The crown prince Zichu succeeded him on the throne and became known as King Zhuangxiang.

sentences.zh: 莊襄王所母華陽后為華陽太后，真母夏姬尊以為夏太后。

translations.literal: King Zhuangxiang's adoptive mother, Queen Huayang, became Empress Dowager Huayang, and his birth mother, Lady Xia, was honored as Empress Dowager Xia.
translations.idiomatic: King Zhuangxiang elevated his adoptive mother, Queen Huayang, to the title of Empress Dowager Huayang, and his birth mother, Lady Xia, was honored as Empress Dowager Xia.

sentences.zh: 莊襄王元年，以呂不韋為丞相，封為文信侯，食河南雒陽十萬戶。

translations.literal: In the first year of King Zhuangxiang, he made Lü Buwei chancellor, enfeoffed him as Marquis Wenxin, and granted him the revenue of one hundred thousand households in Luoyang, Henan.
translations.idiomatic: In the first year of his reign, King Zhuangxiang appointed Lü Buwei as chancellor and enfeoffed him as the Marquis of Wenxin, granting him the revenue from one hundred thousand households in Luoyang, Henan.

translations.literal: Qin king established one year, died, posthumous title Xiaowen Wang. Crown prince Zichu succeeded, this Zhuangxiang Wang. Zhuangxiang Wang birth mother Huayang Queen as Huayang Empress Dowager, true mother Xia Ji honored as Xia Empress Dowager. Zhuangxiang Wang first year, appointed Lü Buwei as chancellor, enfeoffed as Wenxin Marquis, ate Luoyang ten thousand households.

sentences.zh: 莊襄王即位三年，薨，太子政立為王，尊呂不韋為相國，號稱「仲父」。

translations.literal: King Zhuangxiang reigned for three years and died. Crown Prince Zheng was made king, and he honored Lü Buwei as prime minister, calling him "Second Father."
translations.idiomatic: King Zhuangxiang reigned for three years before his death. The crown prince, Zheng, ascended the throne and elevated Lü Buwei to the position of prime minister, addressing him with the honorific "Second Father."

sentences.zh: 秦王年少，太后時時竊私通呂不韋。

translations.literal: The King of Qin was young, and the Queen Dowager from time to time secretly carried on an illicit affair with Lü Buwei.
translations.idiomatic: As the King of Qin was still young, the Queen Dowager secretly resumed her liaison with Lü Buwei from time to time.

sentences.zh: 不韋家僮萬人。

translations.literal: Buwei's household servants numbered ten thousand.
translations.idiomatic: By this time, Lü Buwei's household servants numbered ten thousand.

translations.literal: Zhuangxiang Wang ascended third year, died, crown prince Zheng established as king, honored Lü Buwei as prime minister, titled 'Zhong Fu'. Qin king young, Empress Dowager often secretly communicated Lü Buwei. Buwei household retainers ten thousand persons.

sentences.zh: 當是時，魏有信陵君，楚有春申君，趙有平原君，齊有孟嘗君，皆下士喜賓客以相傾。

translations.literal: At that time, Wei had the Lord of Xinling, Chu had the Lord of Chunshen, Zhao had the Lord of Pingyuan, and Qi had the Lord of Mengchang, all of whom humbled themselves before scholars and delighted in hosting guests to outdo one another.
translations.idiomatic: In that era, the state of Wei had the Lord of Xinling, Chu had the Lord of Chunshen, Zhao had the Lord of Pingyuan, and Qi had the Lord of Mengchang. Each of these great lords vied with the others in humbling himself before scholars and hosting retainers.

sentences.zh: 呂不韋以秦之彊，羞不如，亦招致士，厚遇之，至食客三千人。

translations.literal: Lü Buwei, relying on Qin's power, was ashamed to be outdone, and also recruited scholars and treated them generously, until his retainers numbered three thousand.
translations.idiomatic: Lü Buwei, with the might of Qin behind him, was ashamed to be outdone. He too recruited scholars and treated them generously, until the retainers at his table numbered three thousand.

sentences.zh: 是時諸侯多辯士，如荀卿之徒，著書布天下。

translations.literal: At that time, the feudal lords had many persuasive scholars, such as the followers of Xunzi, who wrote books that spread throughout the realm.
translations.idiomatic: In those days the feudal states boasted many eloquent scholars, men like Xunzi and his followers, who wrote books that circulated throughout the realm.

sentences.zh: 呂不韋乃使其客人人著所聞，集論以為八覽、六論、十二紀，二十餘萬言。

translations.literal: Lü Buwei then had each of his retainers write down what he knew, and collected their essays into the Eight Surveys, the Six Discussions, and the Twelve Almanacs, totaling over two hundred thousand words.
translations.idiomatic: Lü Buwei then instructed each of his retainers to write down his knowledge, and compiled their essays into the Eight Surveys, the Six Discussions, and the Twelve Almanacs, totaling over two hundred thousand characters.

sentences.zh: 以為備天地萬物古今之事，號曰呂氏春秋。

translations.literal: He considered it to encompass all matters of heaven and earth, the myriad things, past and present, and titled it the Spring and Autumn Annals of Master Lü.
translations.idiomatic: He believed the work encompassed all matters of heaven and earth, the myriad things of past and present, and gave it the title The Spring and Autumn Annals of Master Lü.

sentences.zh: 布咸陽市門，懸千金其上，延諸侯游士賓客有能增損一字者予千金。

translations.literal: He displayed it at the market gate of Xianyang, suspended a thousand catties of gold above it, and invited the traveling scholars and retainers of the feudal lords, promising a thousand catties of gold to anyone who could add or remove a single word.
translations.idiomatic: He had the work displayed at the market gate of Xianyang and suspended a thousand catties of gold above it, inviting any traveling scholar or retainer who could add to or remove a single character to claim the gold.

translations.literal: At this time, Wei had Xinyang Jun, Chu had Chunshen Jun, Zhao had Pingyuan Jun, Qi had Mengchang Jun, all recruited scholars delighted guests to mutually contend. Lü Buwei with Qin's strength, ashamed not equal, also recruited scholars, thick treated them, to guests three thousand persons. At this time feudal lords many debaters, like Xun Qing and others, wrote books spread world. Lü Buwei thus made his guests each write what heard, collected theories as eight views, six discussions, twelve records, twenty plus ten thousand words. Considered complete heaven earth myriad things ancient modern affairs, titled 'Lü Shi Chun Qiu'. Spread Xianyang market gate, hung thousand jin above, invited feudal lords wandering scholars guests able increase decrease one word give thousand jin.

sentences.zh: 始皇帝益壯，太后淫不止。

translations.literal: The First Emperor grew ever more mature, but the Queen Dowager's licentiousness did not cease.
translations.idiomatic: As the young king grew into manhood, the Queen Dowager's appetites remained unabated.

sentences.zh: 呂不韋恐覺禍及己，乃私求大陰人嫪毐以為舍人，時縱倡樂，使毐以其陰關桐輪而行，令太后聞之，以啗太后。

translations.literal: Lü Buwei feared that the affair would be discovered and bring disaster upon himself, so he secretly sought out a man named Lao Ai who was greatly endowed, and took him on as a retainer. From time to time he arranged entertainment, having Lao Ai thread his member through a paulownia wheel and walk about with it, so that the Queen Dowager would hear of it and be enticed.
translations.idiomatic: Lü Buwei grew fearful that their affair would be discovered and bring ruin upon him. He secretly sought out a man named Lao Ai, who was remarkably well-endowed, and took him on as a retainer. From time to time he arranged entertainments in which Lao Ai displayed his endowment by threading his member through a paulownia-wood wheel and walking about, ensuring that word of the spectacle would reach the Queen Dowager and entice her.

sentences.zh: 太后聞，果欲私得之。

translations.literal: The Queen Dowager heard of it, and as expected desired to have him secretly.
translations.idiomatic: When the Queen Dowager heard of this, she desired to have Lao Ai for herself, just as Lü Buwei had intended.

sentences.zh: 呂不韋乃進嫪毐，詐令人以腐罪告之。

translations.literal: Lü Buwei then presented Lao Ai, and fraudulently had someone accuse him of a crime deserving castration.
translations.idiomatic: Lü Buwei then presented Lao Ai to her and arranged for someone to bring a false charge against him that carried the punishment of castration.

sentences.zh: 不韋又陰謂太后曰：「可事詐腐，則得給事中。」

translations.literal: Buwei also secretly told the Queen Dowager, "If the castration can be faked, then he can be assigned to serve in the palace."
translations.idiomatic: Lü Buwei also secretly told the Queen Dowager, "If we can fake the castration, he can be assigned to serve inside the palace."

sentences.zh: 太后乃陰厚賜主腐者吏，詐論之，拔其須眉為宦者，遂得侍太后。

translations.literal: The Queen Dowager then secretly and generously bribed the official in charge of castrations, who falsely declared the sentence carried out. His beard and eyebrows were plucked to make him pass as a eunuch, and he was thus able to attend upon the Queen Dowager.
translations.idiomatic: The Queen Dowager secretly bribed the official overseeing the castrations with lavish gifts, and the man falsely certified that the punishment had been carried out. Lao Ai had his beard and eyebrows plucked to pass for a eunuch, and so he entered the palace to attend upon the Queen Dowager.

sentences.zh: 太后私與通，絕愛之。

translations.literal: The Queen Dowager secretly carried on an affair with him and loved him passionately.
translations.idiomatic: The Queen Dowager took Lao Ai as her secret lover and became passionately devoted to him.

sentences.zh: 有身，太后恐人知之，詐卜當避時，徙宮居雍。

translations.literal: When she became pregnant, the Queen Dowager feared discovery and, on the pretext that a divination counseled her to avoid the time, she relocated her palace to Yong.
translations.idiomatic: When she became pregnant, the Queen Dowager feared discovery. On the pretext that a divination had advised her to avoid an inauspicious period, she relocated her residence to the palace at Yong.

sentences.zh: 嫪毐常從，賞賜甚厚，事皆決於嫪毐。

translations.literal: Lao Ai always accompanied her, was rewarded generously, and all decisions were made by Lao Ai.
translations.idiomatic: Lao Ai accompanied her constantly, received lavish rewards, and all matters of state in her household were decided by him.

sentences.zh: 嫪毐家僮數千人，諸客求宦為嫪毐舍人千餘人。

translations.literal: Lao Ai's household servants numbered several thousand, and the guests seeking office who became Lao Ai's retainers exceeded a thousand.
translations.idiomatic: Lao Ai's household servants numbered in the thousands, and over a thousand men seeking government appointments had attached themselves to him as retainers.

translations.literal: First Emperor increasingly mature, Empress Dowager licentious not stop. Lü Buwei feared calamity reach self, thus secretly sought great endowment man Lao Ai as retainer, timely released singers music, made Ai with his endowment pass paulownia wheel to travel, commanded Empress Dowager hear, to tempt Empress Dowager. Empress Dowager heard, truly wished privately obtain him. Lü Buwei thus advanced Lao Ai, falsely had person with rotten crime accuse him. Buwei again secretly told Empress Dowager: 'Can make falsely rotten crime, then obtain serve inner palace.' Empress Dowager thus secretly thick rewarded main rotten officials, falsely judged him, pulled his beard eyebrows as eunuch, thus obtained serve Empress Dowager. Empress Dowager privately communicated, extremely favored him. Had pregnancy, Empress Dowager feared people know, falsely divined should avoid time, moved palace dwell Yong. Lao Ai constantly followed, rewards very thick, affairs all decided by Lao Ai. Lao Ai household retainers several thousand, various guests sought office as Lao Ai retainers thousand plus persons.

sentences.zh: 始皇七年，莊襄王母夏太后薨。

translations.literal: In the seventh year of the First Emperor, Empress Dowager Xia, the mother of King Zhuangxiang, died.
translations.idiomatic: In the seventh year of the First Emperor's reign, Empress Dowager Xia, the mother of King Zhuangxiang, died.

sentences.zh: 孝文王后曰華陽太后，與孝文王會葬壽陵。

translations.literal: The queen of King Xiaowen, called Empress Dowager Huayang, was to be buried with King Xiaowen at the Shouling tomb.
translations.idiomatic: Empress Dowager Huayang, the queen of King Xiaowen, was to be interred with King Xiaowen at the Shouling tomb.

sentences.zh: 夏太后子莊襄王葬芷陽，故夏太后獨別葬杜東，曰「東望吾子，西望吾夫。

translations.literal: Empress Dowager Xia's son, King Zhuangxiang, was buried at Zhiyang. Therefore Empress Dowager Xia was buried separately, east of Du, and said, "To the east I can gaze upon my son, and to the west upon my husband."
translations.idiomatic: Her son, King Zhuangxiang, was buried at Zhiyang. Therefore Empress Dowager Xia was buried separately, east of Du. She had said, "To the east I shall gaze upon my son, and to the west upon my husband."

sentences.zh: 後百年，旁當有萬家邑」。

translations.literal: "A hundred years from now, there should be a city of ten thousand households beside this place."
translations.idiomatic: "A hundred years hence, a city of ten thousand households will rise beside this spot."

translations.literal: First Emperor seventh year, Zhuangxiang Wang mother Xia Empress Dowager died. Xiaowen Wang queen called Huayang Empress Dowager, with Xiaowen Wang met buried Shou Mausoleum. Xia Empress Dowager son Zhuangxiang Wang buried Zhi Yang, thus Xia Empress Dowager alone separately buried Du east, said 'East look my son, west look my husband. After hundred years, beside should have ten thousand household city.'

sentences.zh: 始皇九年，有告嫪毐實非宦者，常與太后私亂，生子二人，皆匿之。

translations.literal: In the ninth year of the First Emperor, someone reported that Lao Ai was in fact not a eunuch, that he regularly had illicit relations with the Queen Dowager, and that they had produced two sons, both of whom they had concealed.
translations.idiomatic: In the ninth year of the First Emperor's reign, someone informed on Lao Ai, revealing that he was not truly a eunuch, that he had been carrying on an illicit affair with the Queen Dowager, and that they had secretly borne two sons, both of whom they had hidden.

sentences.zh: 與太后謀曰「王即薨，以子為後」。

translations.literal: He had plotted with the Queen Dowager, saying, "When the king dies, we will make our son his successor."
translations.idiomatic: He and the Queen Dowager had conspired together, agreeing that when the king died, they would place their son on the throne as his successor.

sentences.zh: 於是秦王下吏治，具得情實，事連相國呂不韋。

translations.literal: Thereupon the King of Qin ordered officials to investigate, and they obtained the full truth of the matter. The case implicated the prime minister, Lü Buwei.
translations.idiomatic: The King of Qin ordered his officials to conduct an investigation, and they uncovered the full truth. The affair implicated the prime minister, Lü Buwei.

sentences.zh: 九月，夷嫪毐三族，殺太后所生兩子，而遂遷太后於雍。

translations.literal: In the ninth month, the three clans of Lao Ai were exterminated, the two sons born of the Queen Dowager were killed, and the Queen Dowager was removed to Yong.
translations.idiomatic: In the ninth month, Lao Ai's three clans were exterminated, the two sons the Queen Dowager had borne him were put to death, and the Queen Dowager herself was banished to Yong.

sentences.zh: 諸嫪毐舍人皆沒其家而遷之蜀。

translations.literal: All of Lao Ai's retainers had their families' property confiscated and were exiled to Shu.
translations.idiomatic: All of Lao Ai's retainers had their households seized and were exiled to the remote region of Shu.

sentences.zh: 王欲誅相國，為其奉先王功大，及賓客辯士為游說者眾，王不忍致法。

translations.literal: The king wished to execute the prime minister, but because his service to the late king had been great, and because his guests and persuasive scholars who lobbied on his behalf were numerous, the king could not bring himself to apply the law.
translations.idiomatic: The king wished to execute the prime minister as well, but Lü Buwei's service to the late king had been too great, and the retainers and eloquent scholars who interceded on his behalf were too numerous. The king could not bring himself to put him to death.

translations.literal: First Emperor ninth year, had accusation Lao Ai truly not eunuch, constantly with Empress Dowager privately chaotic, bore sons two persons, all hid them. With Empress Dowager planned said 'King if dies, with son as successor'. Thus Qin king sent officials investigate, fully obtained real situation, affair connected prime minister Lü Buwei. Ninth month, exterminated Lao Ai three clans, killed Empress Dowager born two sons, then moved Empress Dowager to Yong. Various Lao Ai retainers all confiscated households moved to Shu. King wished execute prime minister, for his serve previous king merit great, and guests debaters for wandering persuasion many, king not bear apply law.

sentences.zh: 秦王十年十月，免相國呂不韋。

translations.literal: In the tenth month of the tenth year of the King of Qin, he dismissed the prime minister Lü Buwei from office.
translations.idiomatic: In the tenth month of the tenth year of the King of Qin's reign, Lü Buwei was dismissed from the office of prime minister.

sentences.zh: 及齊人茅焦說秦王，秦王乃迎太后於雍，歸復咸陽，而出文信侯就國河南。

translations.literal: When the man of Qi, Mao Jiao, remonstrated with the King of Qin, the king then welcomed the Queen Dowager back from Yong and restored her to Xianyang, while he sent the Marquis of Wenxin to take up his fief in Henan.
translations.idiomatic: When Mao Jiao, a man from Qi, remonstrated with the king, the King of Qin relented and welcomed the Queen Dowager back from Yong to Xianyang. He then sent the Marquis of Wenxin to take up residence in his fief in Henan.

translations.literal: Qin king tenth year tenth month, dismissed prime minister Lü Buwei. When Qi person Mao Jiao persuaded Qin king, Qin king then welcomed Empress Dowager at Yong, returned restored Xianyang, yet sent Wenxin Marquis go to fief Henan.

sentences.zh: 歲餘，諸侯賓客使者相望於道，請文信侯。

translations.literal: After more than a year, envoys and retainers from the feudal lords could be seen one after another on the road, all calling upon the Marquis of Wenxin.
translations.idiomatic: After more than a year, a steady stream of envoys and retainers from the feudal lords could be seen traveling the roads, all seeking audience with the Marquis of Wenxin.

sentences.zh: 秦王恐其為變，乃賜文信侯書曰：「君何功於秦？

translations.literal: The King of Qin feared he would cause trouble, and so he sent the Marquis of Wenxin a letter saying, "What service have you rendered to Qin?
translations.idiomatic: The King of Qin feared that Lü Buwei would stir up rebellion. He sent the Marquis of Wenxin a letter that read, "What service have you ever rendered to Qin?

sentences.zh: 秦封君河南，食十萬戶。

translations.literal: Qin enfeoffed you in Henan and granted you the revenue of one hundred thousand households.
translations.idiomatic: Yet Qin enfeoffed you in Henan and granted you the revenue of one hundred thousand households.

sentences.zh: 君何親於秦？

translations.literal: What kinship do you have with Qin?
translations.idiomatic: What kinship do you have with the royal house of Qin?

sentences.zh: 號稱仲父。

translations.literal: Yet you are called 'Second Father.'
translations.idiomatic: Yet you bear the title of 'Second Father.'

sentences.zh: 其與家屬徙處蜀！」

translations.literal: You and your family are to relocate to Shu!"
translations.idiomatic: You and your entire household are to relocate to Shu!"

sentences.zh: 呂不韋自度稍侵，恐誅，乃飲酖而死。

translations.literal: Lü Buwei calculated that the pressure would gradually increase and feared execution, so he drank poison and died.
translations.idiomatic: Lü Buwei judged that the noose was tightening. Fearing execution, he drank poison and died.

sentences.zh: 秦王所加怒呂不韋、嫪毐皆已死，乃皆復歸嫪毐舍人遷蜀者。

translations.literal: Since the ones upon whom the King of Qin had directed his anger, Lü Buwei and Lao Ai, had both already died, he then allowed all of Lao Ai's retainers who had been exiled to Shu to return.
translations.idiomatic: Now that both Lü Buwei and Lao Ai, the targets of the King of Qin's wrath, were dead, the king allowed all of Lao Ai's retainers who had been exiled to Shu to return.

translations.literal: Year plus, various feudal lord guests envoys mutually looked on road, requested Wenxin Marquis. Qin king feared his change, thus granted Wenxin Marquis letter said: 'Lord what merit to Qin? Qin enfeoffed lord Henan, ate ten thousand households. Lord what close to Qin? Titled Zhong Fu. Him with family move dwell Shu!' Lü Buwei self-measured gradually encroached, feared execution, thus drank poison died. Qin king added anger Lü Buwei, Lao Ai all already dead, thus all restored Lao Ai retainers moved Shu ones.

sentences.zh: 始皇十九年，太后薨，謚為帝太后，與莊襄王會葬茝陽。

translations.literal: In the nineteenth year of the First Emperor, the Queen Dowager died, was given the posthumous title Empress Dowager, and was buried with King Zhuangxiang at Zhiyang.
translations.idiomatic: In the nineteenth year of the First Emperor's reign, the Queen Dowager died. She was given the posthumous title of Empress Dowager and was interred beside King Zhuangxiang at Zhiyang.

translations.literal: First Emperor nineteenth year, Empress Dowager died, posthumous title Di Empress Dowager, with Zhuangxiang Wang met buried Zhi Yang.

sentences.zh: 太史公曰：不韋及嫪毐貴，封號文信侯。

translations.literal: The Grand Historian says: Buwei rose to eminence together with Lao Ai, and was enfeoffed as the Marquis of Wenxin.
translations.idiomatic: The Grand Historian remarks: When Lü Buwei rose to power alongside Lao Ai, he was enfeoffed as the Marquis of Wenxin.

sentences.zh: 人之告嫪毐，毐聞之。

translations.literal: When someone informed on Lao Ai, Lao Ai learned of it.
translations.idiomatic: When someone informed against Lao Ai, word of it reached him.

sentences.zh: 秦王驗左右，未發。

translations.literal: The King of Qin investigated those around him but had not yet taken action.
translations.idiomatic: The King of Qin conducted inquiries among those close to the case but had not yet acted.

sentences.zh: 上之雍郊，毐恐禍起，乃與黨謀，矯太后璽發卒以反蘄年宮。

translations.literal: When the king went to the suburban altars at Yong, Lao Ai feared that disaster was about to strike, and so conspired with his confederates, forged the Queen Dowager's seal to mobilize soldiers, and launched a rebellion at the Qinian Palace.
translations.idiomatic: When the king traveled to the suburban altars at Yong for the sacrifices, Lao Ai feared that his downfall was imminent. He conspired with his confederates, forged the Queen Dowager's seal to mobilize troops, and launched a rebellion at the Qinian Palace.

sentences.zh: 發吏攻毐，毐敗亡走，追斬之好畤，遂滅其宗。

translations.literal: Officials were dispatched to attack Lao Ai. Lao Ai was defeated and fled, was pursued and executed at Haozhi, and his entire clan was destroyed.
translations.idiomatic: The king dispatched officials to attack Lao Ai. Lao Ai was defeated and fled, but was pursued and cut down at Haozhi. His entire clan was exterminated.

sentences.zh: 而呂不韋由此絀矣。

translations.literal: And Lü Buwei was thus brought low.
translations.idiomatic: Through these events, Lü Buwei was brought to ruin.

sentences.zh: 孔子之所謂「聞」者，其呂子乎？

translations.literal: The one whom Confucius called a man of 'reputation' rather than true renown — was that not Master Lü?
translations.idiomatic: Was it not Master Lü whom Confucius had in mind when he spoke of those who pursue mere 'reputation' rather than true renown?

translations.literal: Grand Historian says: Buwei and Lao Ai noble, enfeoffed titled Wenxin Marquis. Person accused Lao Ai, Ai heard. Qin king examined attendants, not issued. Went to Yong suburbs, Ai feared calamity rise, thus with party planned, forged Empress Dowager seal sent troops to rebel Qinian Palace. Sent officials attacked Ai, Ai defeated fled, pursued beheaded Haozhi, thus extinguished his clan. Lü Buwei from this demoted. Confucius's so-called 'heard' ones, perhaps Lü Zi?
